claim: AI coding is not a one-shot prompting exercise; it requires iterative collaboration between developer and AI, similar to pair programming with a human partner
actionable: Treat AI coding as a back-and-forth collaborative conversation where you describe the problem, discuss architecture options, and iterate feature-by-feature rather than asking for complete code generation
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Writing a PRD (Product Requirements Document) before coding improves AI assistant performance for larger problems
actionable: For significant projects, write a detailed PRD and include it as part of your initial prompt to the AI to establish clear requirements before iterating on implementation
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Experienced developers should focus prompts on the desired end result rather than the specific implementation approach when using AI
actionable: Step back from prescribing how the AI should solve the problem and instead describe what the final output should look like, giving the model flexibility in implementation
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Taking regular checkpoints every 20-30 minutes when working with AI prevents losing progress if the model goes down wrong paths
actionable: Create checkpoints in your conversation whenever you reach a stable point where things are working; if the model goes wrong, roll back to the checkpoint and start a new conversation from there
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Firebase Studio's auto-agent mode allows non-technical users to apply code changes without understanding the underlying code by automatically executing most changes without manual approval
actionable: Use auto-agent mode in Firebase Studio when working with non-technical collaborators or when you want hands-off automation, but start with step-by-step mode to understand the workflow first
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Clear specification of technologies and frameworks in prompts significantly improves AI output quality for coding tasks
actionable: Explicitly state the technologies you want to use (e.g., 'use React with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS') rather than letting the AI choose, unless you want it to explore alternatives
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Using MCPs (Model Context Providers) with AI coding tools grounds the model in your specific technology stack and project context
actionable: Install relevant MCPs for your frameworks and languages in Firebase Studio or Claude to provide the model with up-to-date documentation and best practices for your specific tech stack
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: AI rules files containing project-specific style guides, technology preferences, and deployment configuration help maintain consistency in AI-generated code
actionable: Create an AI rules file documenting your coding style, technology preferences, and project structure; include it in your prompts or configure it in your AI coding tool
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Different developers have different workflows and preferences; some prefer CLI tools while others prefer UI-focused tools, and both approaches are valid for AI-assisted development
actionable: Experiment with different AI coding tools (CLI-based like Gemini CLI, UI-based like Firebase Studio) to find the workflow that matches your preferences
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Non-traditional coders can create sophisticated applications using AI-assisted tools by focusing on end-user needs rather than implementation details
actionable: If you're new to coding, use AI tools to focus on what you want to build rather than worrying about syntax or technical implementation; this can lead to innovative applications
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly

claim: Firebase Studio's prototype-first experience allows makers to work visually and in code interchangeably, switching between low-code UI and full IDE without losing progress
actionable: Use Firebase Studio's prototype mode for initial visual design, then drill into the code when you need more control; you can switch back and forth as needed
source_title: The Future of AI Coding with Aja Hammerly
